model.label: GPT-5.4
model.description: OpenAI • Frontier GPT model for general-purpose reasoning, tool use, and agentic workflows.

pick.rationale: UCLA is the safer pick because its predictive profile is clearly stronger in both major efficiency systems, especially on defense, while UCF grades more like a fringe top-60 team. The market also supports that gap, with UCLA installed as a meaningful favorite, and I did not find a confirmed availability issue strong enough to change the outlook. UCF has a credible upset path if its offense wins the shot-making battle, but the Bruins still look like the more complete team over 40 minutes.

reasoningStep.title: Compared team quality, health context, and upset path
reasoningStep.summary: UCLA rates materially better than UCF in both KenPom and Torvik, with the biggest edge on defense, and the betting market aligns with that view. Injury-related searches surfaced discussion but no confirmed major absence significant enough to flip the pick. UCF’s offense gives it a real path to an upset, so this is not a lock, but UCLA remains the more reliable advance choice.
evidence: KenPom: UCLA No. 27 (AdjEM 21.64, AdjO 123.8 No. 22, AdjD 102.1) vs UCF No. 54 (AdjEM 15.05, AdjO 120.5 No. 40, AdjD 105.5).
Torvik: UCLA No. 24 (AdjOE 124.50 No. 17, AdjDE 101.74 No. 49) vs UCF No. 58 (AdjOE 120.02 No. 39, AdjDE 105.65 No. 114).
BetMGM/USA Today matchup preview listed UCLA as roughly a 6.5-point favorite.
News and web searches did not reveal a confirmed major injury absence that materially changed the baseline projection.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.text: UCLA vs UCF picks, odds for March Madness NCAA Tournament Skip to main content Home NFL NBA NHL MLB WNBA Outdoors BetFTW Casinos Newsletter Pop Culture College Football Men's College Basketball Women's College Basketball MotorSports Golf USA TODAY Sports UCLA vs UCF picks, predictions, odds for NCAA Tournament March Madness Jeremy Cluff Arizona Republic Show Caption Hide Caption March Madness Final Four predictions and national champion picks From chalk favorites to hot upsets, the panel shares Final Four picks and bold calls on who cuts down the nets. No. 7 seed UCLA and No. 10 seed UCF play Friday, March 20, in a March Madness NCAA Tournament first-round game . The East Region game is scheduled for 4:25 p.m. MST and can be seen on TBS ( stream with Sling TV ). Who will win the March Madness game and advance to the second round in the NCAA Tournament bracket ? Check out these March Madness picks and March Madness predictions for the men's college basketball NCAA Tournament matchup . UCLA is a 6.5-point favorite over UCF in the game in March Madness odds provided by BetMGM Sportsbook . The Bruins are -250 on the moneyline. The Knights are +200. The over/under for the game is set at 152.5 points. March Madness picks: Ohio State-TCU | Nebraska-Troy | Louisville-South Florida | Wisconsin-High Point | Duke-Siena | Vanderbilt-McNeese | Michigan State-North Dakota State | Arkansas-Hawaii | North Carolina-VCU | Saint Mary's-Texas A&M | Illinois-Penn | Georgia-Saint Louis | Gonzaga-Kennesaw State | Houston-Idaho | Kentucky-Santa Clara | Texas Tech-Akron | Arizona-Long Island | Virginia-Wright State | Iowa State-Tennessee State | Alabama-Hofstra | Villanova-Utah State | Clemson-Iowa | St. John's-UNI | UCLA-UCF | Purdue-Queens | Kansas-Cal Baptist | UConn-Furman | Miami-Missouri | The Republic's score predictions Watch UCLA vs UCF on Sling The Arizona Republic : UCLA 82, UCF 77 Jeremy Cluff writes: "This is a tough game to predict, but the Bruins had wins over Nebraska and Michigan State near the end of the season and played Purdue tough in the Big Ten tournament." March Madness picks: Predictions for NCAA Tournament first-round games Dimers : UCLA 79, UCF 72 It writes: "After extensive simulations, our model gives UCF a win probability of 28%, while UCLA has a win probability of 72%." Join the madness: USA TODAY Bracket Challenge | Survivor Pool | Printable bracket ESPN : UCLA has a 72.2% chance to beat UCF The site gives the Knights a 27.8% shot at defeating the Bruins in the NCAA Tournament March Madness first-round game. Bookies.com : Bet UCF with the points vs UCLA Bill Speros writes: "The banged-up Bruins come into this tournament playing their best ball of the season. UCF slugged its way through the Big 12 with an impressive front four. A full-healthy UCLA squad wins this one easily, but the number tells us the Bruins aren't expected to be fully healthy." March Madness odds: Ohio State-TCU | Nebraska-Troy | Louisville-South Florida | Wisconsin-High

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
